public_reputation: First famed road-shield of the present age, remembered for holding the Northroad through a glyph-beast winter.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects readiness without theatricality, as if discipline matters more than spectacle. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a warrior still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of warrior in and around Shelkopolis, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Shelkopolis workable through the obligations of warrior while managing the immediate pressure of glyph corruption beyond the city in Shelk.
hidden_pressure: Must balance deterrence, morale, and actual capability while others often judge only the visible surface. Around Shelkopolis, that usually means carrying glyph corruption beyond the city, trade security obligations, noble rivalry without letting the role of warrior visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Ser Caldrin Vey can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Shelkopolis.
Ser Caldrin Vey will trade useful tactical knowledge if the party solves a security problem too sensitive for ordinary channels.
Ser Caldrin Vey needs help hardening a position, drilling unreliable auxiliaries, or reading an approaching threat around Shelkopolis.
Ser Caldrin Vey needs help with a problem in Shelkopolis that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Ser Caldrin Vey as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence. In Shelkopolis, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Shelk nobility, Roadwardens Order.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shelkopolis: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes
